audit_meta.honest_assessment: Site is growing slowly but steadily — GSC impressions up 53.6% (700 to 1,075), Tier A sessions up 7.2% (83 to 89), bot inflation improved (66% to 57%). Zero clicks remains the story. The FAQPage duplicate on /case-studies/ has been open for 6 consecutive audits with no code fix — this is the single most impactful intervention available. Beyond that, the site needs TIME and authority building, not content. Content pipeline has been stalled 73 days and orbit_3_flow is fully drained. AI citations near-zero (1 Perplexity session). AEO infrastructure gaps exist (no data-ai-summary on commercial pages) but ROI is low until traffic materialises. The most actionable wins are: fix the FAQPage duplicate, submit the 2 stale-empty Bing URLs, and deploy Service schema on the 2 commercial pages that have visible pricing but no JSON-LD.

action_items.description: FAQPage duplicate field error on /case-studies/ — 6th consecutive audit with this bug unresolved. GSC Rich Results verdict: FAIL with 'Duplicate field FAQPage' ERROR on 3 items. The SSG (generate-static.js) correctly injects FAQPage schema on the homepage, but Home.jsx also injects FAQPage via React client-side rendering. When /case-studies/ loads the SPA shell, it inherits the React-injected FAQPage from Home.jsx alongside the SSG-injected schema, creating a duplicate. This blocks FAQ rich results on the highest-engagement commercial page (58.3% engagement rate, 12 sessions). Additionally, the Rich Results test shows cross-property schema leakage — Torxup product images and Memaero drone videos appear in the /case-studies/ schema, indicating the SPA is pulling in unrelated component state.
action_items.prompt: Fix the FAQPage duplicate on launchedin10.co.uk /case-studies/. The root cause is in the React SPA: Home.jsx (or a shared layout component) injects FAQPage JSON-LD client-side, which conflicts with the SSG-injected FAQPage from generate-static.js. Steps: 1) Open the GitHub repo EldrisOU/launchedin10-final. 2) Find where FAQPage schema is injected in the React components (likely src/pages/Home.jsx or src/components/FAQ.jsx or similar). 3) Remove the client-side FAQPage injection — the SSG in generate-static.js already handles this correctly. 4) Also investigate why Torxup and Memaero product/video schema appears on /case-studies/ — this suggests cross-site component leakage in the SPA. 5) After fix, trigger a Cloudflare Pages rebuild and verify via GSC Rich Results test that /case-studies/ no longer shows 'Duplicate field FAQPage'.
action_items.verification: Run gsc_inspect_url on https://launchedin10.co.uk/case-studies/ — Rich Results verdict should change from FAIL to PASS, and 'Duplicate field FAQPage' error should be absent.
action_items.evidence: GSC URL Inspection 01.06.26: Rich Results FAIL — 'Duplicate field FAQPage' on 3 items. Also detected: cross-property schema (Torxup charger image, Memaero drone image, Memaero dust mask video).
action_items.estimated_impact: Unlocks FAQ rich results on /case-studies/ and removes cross-property schema contamination.

action_items.description: Blog post /blog/website-design/web-design-cost-2026-breakdown-small/ has a dateModified of 2026-01-20 — 132 days stale, exceeding the 90-day freshness threshold. This is a commercially important keyword page targeting 'web design cost 2026'. Stale content loses freshness signals in both Google organic and AI citation systems. Additionally, /blog/business-growth/mobile-app-development-company-uk/ is at 85 days — 5 days from tripping the threshold.
action_items.prompt: Refresh the stale blog post on launchedin10.co.uk. In Supabase table li10_posts (project acheexsffdcuzidpiwbh), find the row with slug matching 'web-design-cost-2026-breakdown-small'. Update the content with current 2026 pricing data and market context. Update the modified_at/updated_at timestamp. Trigger SSG rebuild. Also check 'mobile-app-development-company-uk' — if no substantive update is needed, at minimum update the dateModified to reflect a review date. Rebuild and deploy.
action_items.verification: curl the blog post, extract dateModified from JSON-LD. Must be within 7 days of today. Sitemap lastmod must match.
action_items.evidence: Step 5.1: dateModified=2026-01-20 (132 days). Sitemap lastmod=2026-01-20. 90-day threshold breached.
action_items.estimated_impact: Restores freshness signal on commercial keyword page.

action_items.description: BreadcrumbList schema is absent on all 5 commercial pages but present on all blog posts. This is a template-level gap in generate-static.js — the blog post generation includes BreadcrumbList but the commercial page generation does not. BreadcrumbList enables breadcrumb rich results in Google SERPs and improves crawl hierarchy signalling.
action_items.prompt: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD to commercial pages in EldrisOU/launchedin10-final generate-static.js. For each commercial page (homepage, /seo-automation/, /website-translation/, /case-studies/, /blog/), add a BreadcrumbList matching: Home > Page Name. Use the same pattern as the blog post BreadcrumbList generation. Rebuild and deploy.
action_items.verification: Run rich_results_test on each commercial page. BreadcrumbList must be detected without errors.
action_items.evidence: Step 5.2: BreadcrumbList present on 3/8 tested pages (blog only). 0/5 commercial pages have it.
action_items.estimated_impact: Enables breadcrumb rich results on commercial pages.

action_items.description: PageSpeed Insights API returned HTTP 429 (quota exhausted) — unable to measure Core Web Vitals for this audit. This is a portfolio-wide issue affecting all properties. Deferred to next audit or when API quota is restored.
action_items.prompt: Run pagespeed_analyze on https://launchedin10.co.uk/ (mobile) and https://launchedin10.co.uk/seo-automation/ (mobile). Record LCP, CLS, INP, TBT, FCP scores. If API still returns 429, escalate to operator: add a dedicated PageSpeed API key to the GCP project.
action_items.estimated_impact: Establishes CWV baseline for performance tracking.

action_items.description: Monitoring checkpoint for Run 16. Verify: ACT-LI10-023 FAQPage fix deployed. ACT-LI10-029 Bing DocumentSize>0 on /website-translation/ and accessibility-checker. ACT-LI10-028 IndexNow key live. First GSC click. AI citation growth beyond 1 Perplexity session. GSC sitemap last-downloaded date updated (currently 45 days stale at 17.04.26).
action_items.prompt: Run /runperformance launchedin10.co.uk — this is the Run 16 monitoring checkpoint. Key verification items: 1) /case-studies/ Rich Results PASS (ACT-LI10-023). 2) Bing GetUrlInfo DocumentSize>0 for /website-translation/ and accessibility-checker (ACT-LI10-029). 3) IndexNow key file accessible (ACT-LI10-028). 4) GSC impressions trend. 5) Any AI citations beyond Perplexity.
action_items.evidence: Run 15 complete. Next checkpoint warranted in 14 days.
action_items.estimated_impact: Ensures prior actions are implemented and tracks trajectory.

resolved_items.evidence: GSC URL Inspection: Submitted and indexed, crawled 15.05.26. Bing Block A: DocumentSize=14,586.

resolved_items.description: /website-translation/ previously had delayed indexing in Google. Now indexed and crawled 18.05.26.
resolved_items.evidence: GSC URL Inspection: Submitted and indexed, crawled 18.05.26.

resolved_items.description: Bing /blog/ had DocumentSize=0. Now recovered to 55,968 bytes, crawled 17.05.26.

Homepage JSON-LD Offer schema declares prices (£497/£997/£1,997 one-off + £99/£149/£249 monthly) that are NOT visible anywhere on the rendered page. The only price-like text on the homepage is an editorial mention ('You paid £5,000+'). Google's structured data policies require that schema content reflects visible page content — marking up prices not shown to users risks a manual action or automated demotion. The /seo-automation/ and /website-translation/ pages have the inverse problem: visible pricing tiers (£50-£195 and £29.95-£129.95 respectively) but zero JSON-LD Offer/Service schema.
resolved_items.resolution: Executed by ACT Executor agent — batch deployed 01942ce64b347fd471c899b32a042d464076d19a
resolved_items.evidence: Step 5.05B: Homepage JSON-LD has £497/£997/£1997 + monthly prices. Rendered page shows only '£5,000+' editorial text. /seo-automation/ shows £50-£195 visible, 0 JSON-LD. /website-translation/ shows £29.95-£129.95 visible, 0 JSON-LD.

resolved_items.description: data-ai-summary attribute is absent from all 5 commercial pages (homepage, /seo-automation/, /website-translation/, /case-studies/, /blog/ index). Blog posts have 8-9 data-ai-summary blocks each — the SSG blog template includes them. The commercial page templates do not. data-ai-summary is the primary signal for LLM scrapers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot) to identify extractable content blocks. Without it, AI systems must parse the full DOM to find relevant content — reducing citation probability. Given only 1 Perplexity session in 90 days, the AEO ROI is low today, but this is infrastructure that compounds over time.
resolved_items.resolution: Executed by ACT Executor agent — batch deployed 01942ce64b347fd471c899b32a042d464076d19a
resolved_items.evidence: Step 5.05C: 0 data-ai-summary blocks on all 5 commercial pages. Blog posts have 8-9 each.

resolved_items.description: Bing stale-empty-crawl on /website-translation/ (DocumentSize=0 since 08.05.26, 24 days) and /blog/website-design/accessibility-checker-website-essential/ (DocumentSize=0 since 01.05.26, 31 days). Both URLs are IsPage=true in Bing (indexed) but have zero cached content — they are invisible to ChatGPT and Copilot retrieval. /website-translation/ is a core commercial page and the only page ever cited by Perplexity (first citation at Run 14). The accessibility-checker post is the highest-traffic blog post by organic sessions. These 2 pages represent the strongest candidates for AI citation capture and are blocked from the Bing-to-ChatGPT pipeline.
resolved_items.prompt: Submit the 2 stale-empty-crawl URLs to Bing for re-crawl. Run: curl -s -X POST 'https://ssl.bing.com/webmaster/api.svc/json/SubmitUrlBatch?apikey=30fa478ccc434486be022a3e6a513dca' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"siteUrl":"https://launchedin10.co.uk/","urlList":["https://launchedin10.co.uk/website-translation/","https://launchedin10.co.uk/blog/website-design/accessibility-checker-website-essential/"]}'. Expected response: {"d":null} = success. Verify after 7 days by running GetUrlInfo on both URLs — DocumentSize should be >0.
resolved_items.verification: Run Bing GetUrlInfo on both URLs after 7 days. DocumentSize must be >0 for each. /website-translation/ should be >10KB, accessibility-checker should be >15KB based on page content volume.
resolved_items.evidence: Bing Block A 01.06.26: /website-translation/ discovered=08.05.26, crawled=08.05.26, DocumentSize=0, IsPage=true. /blog/.../accessibility-checker.../ discovered=01.05.26, crawled=01.05.26, DocumentSize=0, IsPage=true.
resolved_items.estimated_impact: Unlocks 2 pages for ChatGPT/Copilot retrieval. /website-translation/ was the first-ever Perplexity-cited page.
resolved_items.resolution: Executed by ACT Executor agent — batch deployed via gh-api

resolved_items.description: IndexNow key verification file is not deployed for launchedin10.co.uk. The submit-bing-indexnow.js script in the repo references key '40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e' but the corresponding key file (https://launchedin10.co.uk/40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e.txt) has not been verified as accessible. Without a working IndexNow key file, Bing IndexNow pings return HTTP 422 and URLs are not fast-tracked for crawl. This blocks the Bing-to-ChatGPT pipeline acceleration. Carried forward from Run 14.
resolved_items.prompt: Deploy the IndexNow key verification file for launchedin10.co.uk. In EldrisOU/launchedin10-final: 1) Check if public/40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e.txt exists. If not, create it with content '40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e'. 2) Deploy to Cloudflare Pages. 3) Verify: curl -s https://launchedin10.co.uk/40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e.txt should return the key string. 4) Test IndexNow ping: curl -s -w '\nHTTP %{http_code}' -X POST 'https://api.indexnow.org/indexnow' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"host":"launchedin10.co.uk","key":"40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e","keyLocation":"https://launchedin10.co.uk/40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e.txt","urlList":["https://launchedin10.co.uk/"]}'. Expected: HTTP 200.
resolved_items.verification: curl https://launchedin10.co.uk/40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e.txt returns 200 with key content. IndexNow test ping returns HTTP 200.
resolved_items.evidence: CONTEXT.md: launchedin10.co.uk IndexNow key = NOT DEPLOYED. submit-bing-indexnow.js references key 40b3efc5ab8e4c0e9ca6163daca5d29e.
resolved_items.estimated_impact: Enables Bing fast-crawl pipeline. New content reaches Bing index in minutes instead of days.
resolved_items.resolution: Executed by ACT Executor agent — batch deployed via gh-api
